title: Immediate Family and Police Station Representation
metaTitle: Can Immediate Family Instruct a Solicitor?
metaDescription: Who may instruct a police station solicitor on someone else's behalf — immediate family, current custody only, and detainee consent.
contentHtml:
Who can instruct a police station solicitor?

If someone you care about is in police custody right now at a Kent station, you may be able to help them get a solicitor — but the rules are strict. This article explains who can instruct, what consent is required, and what we cannot help with.

Immediate family only

Under PACE Code C, a solicitor may take instructions from a third party in limited circumstances. In practice, we accept instructions from immediate family: a parent, spouse or civil partner, child, or sibling of the detainee. Extended family, friends, colleagues, and neighbours cannot instruct on someone else's behalf — even if they are trying to help.

Current custody only

We act on immediate matters: someone who is in custody now, or a voluntary interview that is already scheduled. We do not help with arrests from yesterday, people who have already been released, or general "what happened to them" enquiries. Police cannot confirm whether someone is detained — that is a confidentiality rule, not something we can bypass.

The detainee must confirm

Even when immediate family contact us, the detainee must confirm they want the solicitor when the police put that question to them. We cannot act without that confirmation. Tell custody staff you want Tuckers Solicitors LLP and may request Robert Cashman as the attending solicitor.

What to do if you are immediate family

  1. Telephone 01732 247427 — do not rely on email for urgent custody.
  2. Have the detainee's full name and, if known, which Kent station they are at.
  3. Explain your relationship (parent, spouse, child, or sibling).

What we do not do

  • Status checks or welfare updates about someone in custody
  • Missing-person or tracing enquiries
  • Instructions from friends or non-immediate family
  • General legal advice by phone for past events

Further reading

FAQ: immediate custody only · If a loved one is arrested · Kent custody resource hub

General information only — not legal advice for a specific case. Legal services through Tuckers Solicitors LLP (SRA 127795).

faq.question: Can a friend instruct a solicitor for someone in custody?
faq.answer: No. Friends and non-immediate family cannot instruct on someone else's behalf. Immediate family (parent, spouse, child, or sibling) may contact us when someone is in current custody.

faq.question: Can I get an update on whether someone is in custody?
faq.answer: No. Police do not confirm detention details to third parties, and we do not provide status updates. If they are in custody now, immediate family may instruct a solicitor.

author: Robert Cashman
